title: D&D 2024 Species Variant (Lineage)
description: Schema for files in data/species_variants/ (also called lineages). A variant layers additional traits on a parent species; the parent's name is recorded in parent_species and resolved by the data loader. Phase 9 (D1-1) forbids top-level convenience keys for darkvision and speed — these must be encoded as ``grant_darkvision``/``increase_speed`` effects on the relevant trait. ``cantrip_replacement`` and ``spellcasting_ability_choice`` remain as structural lineage metadata: they constrain wizard prompts (cantrip swap source, spell ability choice) rather than granting a sheet-affecting mechanical benefit, and have no equivalent effect type today.

spells_by_level.description: Legacy convenience map (cantrip/3/5/...). Canonical grant is grant_cantrip/grant_spell effects inside the trait. Phase 9 deleted these blocks from the elf lineages; the key is kept in the schema for back-compat on saved data.

cantrip_replacement.description: Structural lineage metadata. Names the class spell list from which the variant's cantrip may be swapped on long rest (e.g. High Elf -> Wizard). Constrains the cantrip-replacement prompt and has no sheet-affecting effect type.

spellcasting_ability_choice.description: Structural lineage metadata. If present, the player chooses one of these abilities as the spellcasting ability for variant-granted spells. Constrains a wizard prompt; not a sheet effect.
